title: Maternity Benefits and Literacy
focus: Covers the Maternity Benefit Act and the status of literacy in India according to PLFS 2023-24.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 072]
8.13. MATERNITY LEAVE
Why in news?
Recently, Supreme Court in Umadevi v. Government of Tamil Nadu, 2025 ruled that maternity leave is an essential component of a woman’s reproductive rights, thereby reinforcing constitutional and human rights of working women.
Key Highlights of Judgment
- Constitutional Protection: A woman’s right to reproductive choices is protected under Article 21, as a part of personal liberty (Suchita Srivastava v. Chandigarh Administration case).
- Human Rights: Reproductive rights are enshrined in Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) & include rights to health, privacy, dignity, and equality.
Maternity Benefit Act, 1961
- It provides paid maternity leave to women workers.
- Applicability: Factories, mines, plantations, government establishments, shops and other workplaces with 10 or more employees. Women covered under Employees’ State Insurance Act, 1948 also receive maternity benefits.
- Maternity Benefit (Amendment) Act, 2017 brought following changes:
- Increased paid maternity leave from 12 weeks to 26 weeks for women with less than two surviving children.
- Out of these 26 weeks, not more than 8 weeks shall be preceding the expected date of delivery.
- Depending upon the nature of work, Act provides for work from home for nursing mothers.
- Extension of 12 weeks of maternity benefit to women who are expecting after having 2 children, commissioning mothers and adopting (child below 3 months) mother.
- Applicable to all establishments employing 10 or more women.
- Any establishment which has 50 or more employees shall have crèche facility.
- Section 11A states that employer shall allow 4 visits a day to creche by woman employee.
8.14. ULLAS - NAV BHARAT SAAKSHARTA KARYAKRAM
Why in the News?
Recently, Tripura becomes third state in country, after Mizoram and Goa, to achieve Full Functional Literacy under ULLAS – Nav Bharat Saaksharta Karyakram (New India Literacy Programme).
More on the News
- Earlier in 2024, Ladakh became the first administrative unit to declare full functional literacy.
- Functional literacy is the ability to read, write, and comprehend information in a way that allows an individual to function effectively in...
